text: Had lunch with Sarah yesterday, and she didn't ask about my week. Guess it's just another example of how I'm not important to her.
It always feels like something is off when we're at the office. Maybe it's just me, but I get this knot in my stomach whenever John walks by.
I was at the grocery store, and they were out of my favorite kind of milk. Seemed really inconvenient. Guess the universe is trying to tell me something.
I love how my best friend always makes time for our weekly calls. It's a good thing he doesn't have other priorities. Like family or work.
When I'm walking down the street, and people don't make eye contact, it feels like they're judging me.
Why do we even bother with networking events if nobody actually remembers who you are? It's all just superficial.
My mom always says to 'not take things personally,' but how can I not when my coworker gives me side-eye during meetings?
I've been meaning to ask Alex about his project, but every time I bring it up, he changes the subject. Maybe he doesn't want me to know something.
Sometimes I get this feeling like people are watching me from across the room. Maybe I'm just paranoid.
I love how my partner remembers all the little things that make me happy. It's a good thing they're always on top of things.
Why do we even go to social gatherings if nobody actually talks to each other? Just standing around, pretending.
Maybe it's just because I'm tired, but every little thing seems like an emergency right now.

When my teacher assigned the project, they didn't explain the rubric very clearly. Maybe that's on purpose, to make us struggle.

I love how my friends always offer help when I need it. It's a good thing we have each other.

I love how my partner always knows what I need before I even ask. It's a good thing they're so intuitive.

Sometimes I get this feeling like people are waiting for me to make the first move in conversations. Maybe I'm just too shy.

Why do we always have to explain ourselves to others? Can't they just trust us?
When I'm at a party, and people are laughing and having fun, it feels like I'm the only one who's not invited to the joke.
I love how my friends always make time for our spontaneous get-togethers. It's a good thing we have each other.
Why do people never want to listen to me when I try to talk about what's really going on?

Why do people always assume the worst about me? Don't they know what I'm really capable of?
I love how my partner always supports me in everything I do. It's a good thing we have each other's backs.

When I'm at home, and there's an empty space on the wall where I was planning to hang something, it feels like a sign that everything is falling apart.
I love how my friends always know when to show up for me. It's a good thing we have each other's schedules coordinated.
Sometimes I get this feeling like people are trying to manipulate me with their words. Maybe I'm just too trusting.
Why do we always have to justify ourselves to others? Can't they just trust us?
When I'm at the office, and people are making plans without me, I get this anxiety that I'm not important enough.

Today's meeting was a disaster, just like every other one this week.
I'm so sick of these cold winter mornings, it's always going to be grey and miserable.
Why did I even bother trying out for the team? They must have known I'd mess up anyway.
What if my boss sees this email mistake? Will he think I'm incompetent?
Ugh, I just got a parking ticket. This is all I need, another expense to worry about.
I've been making excuses for not exercising for months now... and what's the result? My energy levels are always so low.
My sister thinks I should be more confident in my abilities, but she doesn't know how hard it is to put myself out there.
Why do I even bother putting on a smile? It just hides the truth – nobody really likes me.
Have you ever noticed how hard it is to fall asleep when you're stressed about work? It's like your brain is always on high alert.
I'm already imagining all the ways this new project will go wrong... can't I just enjoy the planning process for once?
Why do I keep getting these anxiety attacks in the middle of meetings? What am I doing wrong?
Will my landlord raise the rent again if I don't pay on time? This is going to be a disaster.
I'm starting to feel like a failure as an artist. No matter how hard I try, nobody seems to appreciate my work.
Why do people always ask me for advice when they know I've never experienced this situation before?
I've been putting off that phone call with my ex... but what if it's even worse than I expect?

Sometimes I wonder why I even bother getting up in the morning. Is it really worth all this stress?

I keep thinking about what would happen if my car breaks down on the highway... it's a nightmare scenario.
Maybe if I just stayed in bed today, everything would magically work itself out. Wouldn't that be nice?
Mornings like today are just setting the tone for a chaotic day ahead.
I'm convinced that my manager is going to cut my project short. The way she looks at me sometimes, it's like I can see her thinking 'you're not doing this right'.
I hate walking into meetings when there's already been discussion without me. It makes me wonder what's being said behind closed doors.
Sometimes I feel a knot in my stomach before big presentations, and I'm sure something will go wrong.

When the clock strikes 5 PM on Fridays, I'm sure my team is relieved to be leaving behind my lack of productivity.

When the meeting ends, and everyone starts whispering, I just know they're talking about me.

Why do I feel this sense of foreboding when working on new tasks? It feels like something is bound to go wrong.
I'm sure my team knows more than they're letting on about the changes we've been discussing. They must be planning a secret move.
When I see people glancing at their watches, it makes me think they're waiting for an excuse to leave.

I always seem to feel this weight of responsibility on my shoulders when dealing with high-stakes clients.
Why do I get the feeling that people are just humoring me when they agree with me? They must be waiting for their turn to speak.
The air seemed charged during our last team meeting. It felt like someone was going to bring up a contentious issue.
I've noticed that my manager often has these one-on-one meetings with our clients. She must be trying to cover something up.
Sometimes I feel like I'm walking on eggshells around my colleagues, waiting for them to criticize me.
When people start whispering in meetings, I just know they're talking about me or gossiping behind my back.
The boss always seems so calm and collected before major announcements. That usually means someone's going to get caught off guard.
Why do I feel like everyone is waiting with bated breath for something catastrophic to happen?
I'm sure my team is secretly planning something without telling me, just like they did last time.
When people start avoiding eye contact with me, it makes me wonder if they're hiding something or gossiping behind my back.
The air was thick with tension when our client raised concerns about our services. It felt like someone was going to get reprimanded.
Why do I always feel this sense of impending doom before big meetings? It feels like something is bound to go wrong.
